changes_description: Added a polish phase that perturbs around the current best and re-optimizes with tighter ftol and higher maxiter; also expanded phase-1 perturbation scales and added an additional wider-scale restart. This mirrors the successful 0.9971 approach with an extra seed mix.

metadata.changes: Added a polish phase that perturbs around the current best and re-optimizes with tighter ftol and higher maxiter; also e

blocks.text: The parent configuration scores 0.9963, but the top performer hits 0.9971 with a polish phase applied. I should adopt that polish phase from the better solution as a direct improvement, since it's a proven way to boost the score.

print(type(code), len(code) if code else "none")

blocks.text: I'm going to try a different approach by building directly on the parent solution with a score of 0.9963. I'll add a polish phase from Program 1 and introduce some variation through different RNG seeds to explore the search space more effectively.

tool_input.changes_description: Added a polish phase that perturbs around the current best and re-optimizes with tighter ftol and higher maxiter; also expanded phase-1 perturbation scales and added an additional wider-scale restart. This mirrors the successful 0.9971 approach with an extra seed mix.

summary.approach: Extended multi-restart SLSQP with an additional perturbation scale (0.18) in phase 1 and a 6-restart polish phase around the best solution, using tighter ftol=1e-11 and maxiter=500
summary.key_insight: Adding a polish phase that perturbs around the current best solution (scales 0.002–0.06) and re-optimizes provides incremental improvement over pure hex-lattice restarts, lifting score from 0.9963 to 0.9969
next_directions: Try known near-optimal packings for n=26 from the Packomania database as initial conditions instead of the hex lattice, which may provide a fundamentally better basin of attraction
Use a two-phase approach: first optimize equal radii (single variable r) with free centers, then switch to non-uniform radii optimization, which may converge to a better solution
Increase the number of polish restarts or use a finer grid of perturbation scales (e.g., 10-20 scales from 0.001 to 0.1) to more thoroughly explore the neighborhood of the best solution
avoid: Single-start SLSQP from hex lattice (score 0.9929) — strictly dominated by multi-restart version

reps_annotations.hypothesis: 5x5 grid + 1 gap-filler warm start with SLSQP and analytical jacobians reaches 0.999956 consistently, remains the dominant pattern
